title: ExternalResearchReport
description: Structured outside-in research report on a target company. Canonical contract used by both the Claude external-researcher sub-agent and the Parallel.ai Task API (Deep Research). Designed to be the JSON analog of the markdown report at runs/<slug>/external_report.md.

company.description: Canonical company name as researched (e.g., 'American Electric Power Company, Inc.').

company_slug.description: Kebab-case slug used for output paths (e.g., 'american-electric-power').

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the report was generated.

researcher.description: Identifier of the producing researcher (e.g., 'claude-external-researcher' or 'parallel-task-pro').

tldr.description: 3-5 single-sentence bullets capturing the most important outside-in findings.

company_basics.description: What the company does, its segments, and its business model.

text.description: 2-4 sentence outside-in summary suitable for a deck context slide.

products_services.description: Major product/service lines or business segments.

customer_segments.description: Who the company serves (e.g., 'residential ratepayers', 'large industrial customers', 'commercial PBMs').

text.description: How the company makes money (e.g., 'regulated rate base + transmission ROE', 'capitated dialysis services').

scale_snapshot.description: Quantitative scale. Use 'not_disclosed' for value when genuinely unavailable rather than guessing.

value.description: Free-text description of geographic footprint (e.g., '11-state US service territory: OH, IN, KY, MI, OK, TN, TX, VA, WV, AR, LA').

operating_model_signals.description: Functions/processes/org structure inferable from public sources. Each entry attributes the signal to specific evidence (job posts, press, conference talks, podcasts).

signal.description: What the public evidence tells us about how this function operates.

tech_stack_signals.description: Named systems and vendors with source attribution. Look at case studies, RFPs, job descriptions, GitHub orgs, BuiltWith.

system.description: Vendor or product name (e.g., 'Salesforce Service Cloud', 'SAP S/4HANA', 'Snowflake').

recent_strategic_moves.description: Events from the last 12-18 months only. Older context belongs in company_basics. Includes strategic announcements, M&A, leadership changes, capital allocation, regulatory milestones, partnerships, and public initiatives.

date.description: Date the move was announced. YYYY-MM-DD if known; YYYY-MM if only month is known; YYYY if only year.

public_pain_points.description: Themes from Glassdoor, Trustpilot, Reddit, app stores, regulatory actions, customer complaints. Patterns require multiple independent sources — flag prevalence honestly.

theme.description: The pain point in 1 phrase (e.g., 'long claims-resolution turnaround', 'storm-response communication gaps').

source_pattern.description: Where the signal shows up (e.g., 'recurring Glassdoor reviews from CSRs', 'state PUC complaints', 'app-store reviews 1-star bucket').

prevalence.description: Honest read: 'isolated' = a few mentions, 'recurring' = consistent theme across 5+ sources, 'widespread' = dominant theme in the source pool.

representative_quotes.description: 1-3 representative quotes or excerpts that capture the theme.

open_questions.description: Things the public web couldn't answer that the internal report (or human follow-up) might. Plain strings — no citations expected.

citations.description: All sources referenced by citation_indices anywhere in the document. Order is significant: indices are 0-based positions into this array.

id.description: 0-based index that matches position in this array. Redundant with array position but kept explicit for downstream consumers and human review.

source_type.description: primary = company itself or regulator/court; secondary = reputable journalism / analyst report; aggregator = listicle, Wikipedia, content farm. Used by the judge to score citation quality.

source_date.description: When the underlying content was published. Prefer YYYY-MM-DD when known; partial dates like 'YYYY-MM', 'YYYY-Q3', or 'YYYY' are acceptable. Empty string if genuinely unknown.

date_accessed.description: When the researcher retrieved the source (YYYY-MM-DD).

value.description: Value with units (e.g., '$19.7B', '~17,300', '5.6M'). Use the literal string 'not_disclosed' if genuinely unavailable rather than guessing.

year.description: Year the value applies to (e.g., '2024'). Empty string if not applicable.

rationale.description: 1-2 sentences on why they compete (overlap in product, geography, or customer).
